<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

Now I have a good understanding of the project:

1. **Tech Stack**: React + Vite + TypeScript, vanilla CSS (no Tailwind)
2. **Current UI structure**:
   - `ScreenFrame` - outer shell with gradient background, grid pattern
   - `screen-card` - main content container with glassmorphism style
   - Various screens: TitleScreen, MapScreen, BattleScreen, ResultScreen, StartScreen, EventScreen, RecruitScreen
   - Design tokens: dark theme (#050816, #f4f7fb), blue accents (#90a3ff, #a9b7ff), border radius 24px/18px
   - BEM-like class naming (screen-card__eyebrow, data-panel__title)

3. **Implement-design skill requirements**:
   - Need Figma URL with fileKey and nodeId

```json
{
  "plan": "# Figma 设计全局替换无限世界 UI\n\n## 设计源信息\n\n- **链接**: https://www.figma.com/make/ilCxEbRuCXAOOLeTIAI58Z/Untitled\n- **fileKey** (Make 文件): &#96;ilCxEbRuCXAOOLeTIAI58Z&#96;\n- **范围**: 全局替换（全游戏 UI）\n\n## 现有项目结构\n\n- **技术栈**: React 18 + Vite + TypeScript，**无 Tailwind**，使用 [src/styles/globals/global.css](g:\\codex\\无限世界\\src\\styles\\globals\\global.css) 的 vanilla CSS\n- **布局 shell**: [ScreenFrame](g:\\codex\\无限世界\\src\\components\\layout\\ScreenFrame.tsx) 提供背景和内容容器\n- **7 个屏幕**: TitleScreen、StartScreen、MapScreen、EventScreen、RecruitScreen、BattleScreen、ResultScreen\n\n## 实施流程\n\n### 阶段 1：获取 Figma 设计结构\n\n1. **探测 Make 文件结构**\n   - 调用 &#96;get_metadata(fileKey=\"ilCxEbRuCXAOOLeTIAI58Z\", nodeId=\"0:1\")&#96; 获取页面/节点树\n   - 识别主画布、各页面、组件与设计 token（如有）\n\n2. **获取设计上下文与截图**\n   - 对顶层画布/页面调用 &#96;get_design_context&#96; 和 &#96;get_screenshot&#96;\n   - 若设计分多页/多 frame，则对每个关键 frame 分别调用\n   - 下载并放置 Figma 返回的图片/图标（使用 MCP 提供的 localhost 资源 URL，不引入新图标库）\n\n3. **提取设计规范**\n   - 颜色（背景、文字、强调、边框）\n   - 字体、字号、行高、字重\n   - 圆角、间距、阴影、模糊\n\n### 阶段 2：建立设计 token 与基础样式\n\n4. **更新 [global.css](g:\\codex\\无限世界\\src\\styles\\globals\\global.css)**\n   - 在 &#96;:root&#96; 中定义 Figma 设计 token（颜色、字体、间距等）\n   - 覆盖 &#96;.screen-shell&#96;、&#96;.screen-card&#96;、&#96;.primary-button&#96;、&#96;.secondary-button&#96; 等现有类\n   - 保持现有 BEM 命名，仅替换样式值\n\n5. **更新 [ScreenFrame](g:\\codex\\无限世界\\src\\components\\layout\\ScreenFrame.tsx)**\n   - 按 Figma 背景/布局规范调整背景和内容容器\n   - 保持组件接口不变\n\n### 阶段 3：逐屏替换 UI\n\n6. **TitleScreen**\n   - 按 Figma 布局与样式重做标题、描述、按钮区\n   - 保持 &#96;onStart&#96;、&#96;onDeleteSave&#96;、&#96;hasSave&#96; 等 props 和逻辑不变\n\n7. **StartScreen**\n   - 重做新局初始化界面布局与视觉\n   - 保持 &#96;onStartRun&#96;、&#96;onBackToTitle&#96; 逻辑不变\n\n8. **MapScreen**\n   - 重做「本局概览」「队伍」「地图节点」「当前选择」等面板\n   - 保持数据绑定与交互逻辑\n\n9. **EventScreen / RecruitScreen**\n   - 重做事件/招募选项列表与按钮\n   - 保持 &#96;onChoose&#96; 及条件判断逻辑\n\n10. **BattleScreen**\n    - 重做我方/敌方信息、行动区、目标选择、战斗日志\n    - 保持 &#96;onSubmitAction&#96;、&#96;onBackToMap&#96;、&#96;onFleeBattle&#96; 逻辑不变\n\n11. **ResultScreen**\n    - 重做本局摘要、节点结果、队伍、资源等\n    - 保持 &#96;onReturnToTitle&#96; 逻辑不变\n\n### 阶段 4：验证与收尾\n\n12. **对照 Figma 校验**\n    - 布局（间距、对齐、尺寸）\n    - 字体与颜色\n    - 按钮 hover/disabled 等状态\n    - 响应式（现有 breakpoints 640px / 800px）\n\n13. **可访问性与代码质量**\n    - 确保对比度满足 WCAG\n    - 保持 TypeScript 类型与 JSDoc 注释\n\n## 实施原则\n\n- **Figma 输出（多为 React+Tailwind）为参考**，不直接照搬：项目无 Tailwind，需转换为 vanilla CSS 或内联样式\n- **复用现有类名**：沿用 &#96;screen-card&#96;、&#96;data-panel&#96;、&#96;kv-list&#96; 等，减少改动范围\n- **逻辑与数据流不变**：只替换结构和样式，不改变 props、事件处理或 store 调用\n- **资源来源**：所有视觉资源使用 Figma MCP 返回的 localhost 或下载 URL，不新增图标/字体包（除非设计稿明确要求）\n\n## 依赖与前置条件\n\n- Figma MCP 已连接并可使用 &#96;get_design_context&#96;、&#96;get_metadata&#96;、&#96;get_screenshot&#96;\n- 设计稿中包含与「标题页、新局、地图、事件、招募、战斗、结算」对应的界面或可映射的组件\n- 若 Make 文件内没有明确 node-id，需先用 &#96;get_metadata&#96; 探索节点结构，再选取对应 nodeId 调用 &#96;get_design_context&#96;\n\n## 风险与应对\n\n| 风险 | 应对 |\n|------|------|\n| Figma Make 文件结构与 Design 不同 | 先用 &#96;get_metadata&#96; 探测，必要时查阅 Figma Make 文档 |\n| 设计稿与 7 个屏幕不能一一对应 | 优先实现能直接对应的，其余按 token 和风格做合理适配 |\n| 设计过于复杂导致单个 get_design_context 超时 | 按 frame 分批获取，或使用 &#96;get_metadata&#96; 定位子节点再分次获取 |",
  "name": "Figma 设计全局替换",
  "overview": "将 Figma Make 设计稿 (ilCxEbRuCXAOOLeTIAI58Z) 作为无限世界 UI 的视觉规范，全面替换现有界面，涵盖布局、主题、组件样式和 7 个屏幕的 UI。"
}
```

Result:

```json
{
  "uri": "file://figma/docs/tools-and-prompts.md",
  "mimeType": "text/markdown",
  "text": "# Tools and prompts\n\nThe Figma MCP server provides the following tools:\n\n- [&#96;generate_figma_design&#96;](#generate_figma_design) (specific clients only, remote only): Generates design layers from interfaces\n- [&#96;get_design_context&#96;](#get_design_context): Get the design context for a layer or selection\n- [&#96;get_variable_defs&#96;](#get_variable_defs): Returns the variables and styles used in your Figma selection\n- [&#96;get_code_connect_map&#96;](#get_code_connect_map): Retrieves a mapping between Figma node IDs and their corresponding code components in your codebase\n- [&#96;add_code_connect_map&#96;](#add_code_connect_map): Adds a mapping between a Figma node ID and its corresponding code component in your codebase\n- [&#96;get_screenshot&#96;](#get_screenshot): Allows the agent to take a screenshot of your selection\n- [&#96;create_design_system_rules&#96;](#create_design_system_rules): Creates a rule file that provide agents with the right context to translate designs into frontend code\n- [&#96;get_metadata&#96;](#get_metadata): Returns a sparse XML representation of your selection that contains basic properties such as layer IDs, names, types, position and sizes\n- [&#96;get_figjam&#96;](#get_figjam): Converts FigJam diagrams (such as app architecture workflows) to XML\n- [&#96;generate_diagram&#96;](#generate_diagram): Generates a FigJam diagram from Mermaid syntax\n- [&#96;whoami&#96;](#whoami-remote-only) (remote only): Returns the identity of the user that's authenticated to Figma\n- [&#96;get_code_connect_suggestions&#96;](#get_code_connect_suggestions): A Figma-prompted tool call to find suggestions for mapping Figma node IDs to their corresponding code components in your codebase using Code Connect\n- [&#96;send_code_connect_mappings&#96;](#send_code_connect_mappings): A Figma-prompted tool used after calling &#96;get_code_connect_suggestions&#96; to confirm the suggested Code Connect mappings\n\n## generate_figma_design (remote only) \\{#generate_figma_design}\n\n:::note\n**Note**:\n\n- For use with [select MCP clients](code-to-canvas.md).\n- Remote Figma MCP server only.\n- This tool is exempt from the standard rate limits for the Figma MCP server.\n:::\n\n**Supported file types:** Figma Design\n\nFor detailed instructions, see [Code to canvas](code-to-canvas.md).\n\n&#96;generate_figma_design&#96; lets you prompt your MCP client to send live UI for your web apps and sites as design layers to:\n\n* New Figma Design files\n* Existing Figma Design files\n* Your clipboard\n\n&#96;generate_figma_design&#96; respects your seat type when creating or editing files. New files are created in your team or organization drafts. For existing files, you must have edit permissions for the file.\n\n## get_design_context\n\n**Supported file types:** Figma Design, Figma Make\n\nUse the MCP server to get the [design context](server-returning-web-code.md#about-the-get_design_context-tool) for a layer or your selection in Figma. By default, the output is **React + Tailwind**, but you can customize it through your prompt.\n\n**Suggested prompts:**\n\n- **Change the framework**\n  - &#96;generate my Figma selection in Vue&#96;\n  - &#96;generate my Figma selection in plain HTML + CSS&#96;\n  - &#96;generate my Figma selection in iOS&#96;\n- **Use your components**\n  - &#96;generate my Figma selection using components from src/components/ui&#96;\n  - \\*Tip: set up Code Connect for best code reuse results. Code Connect lets you set up multiple connections per Figma Library. You can map, for example, both your React and SwiftUI code to your Figma components. The Desktop MCP server will use the Code Connect mapping you have selected in Dev Mode. To control which Code Connect mappings are sent via the Remote MCP Server, instruct your agent to set the &#96;clientFrameworks&#96; tool call paramater to the exact Code Connect label you have set up for your mappings, e.g.. &#96;React&#96;, &#96;SwiftUI&#96;.\n- **Combine both**\n  - &#96;generate my Figma selection using components from src/ui and style with Tailwind&#96;\n\n:::note\n**Note:** Selection-based prompting only works with the desktop MCP server. The remote server requires a link to a frame or layer to extract context.\n:::\n\n## get_variable_defs\n\n**Supported file types:** Figma Design\n\nReturns the variables and styles used in your Figma selection (such as colors, spacing, typography).\n\n**You can ask it to:**\n\n- **List all tokens used**\n  - &#96;get the variables used in my Figma selection&#96;\n- **Focus on a specific type**\n  - &#96;what color and spacing variables are used in my Figma selection?&#96;\n- **Get both names and values**\n  - &#96;list the variable names and their values used in my Figma selection&#96;\n\n## get_code_connect_map\n\n**Supported file types:** Figma Design\n\nRetrieves a mapping between Figma node IDs and their corresponding code components in your codebase. Specifically, it returns an object where each key is a Figma node ID, and the value contains:\n\n- &#96;codeConnectSrc&#96;: The location of the component in your codebase (e.g., a file path or URL).\n- &#96;codeConnectName&#96;: The name of the component in your codebase.\n\nThis mapping is used to connect Figma design elements directly to their React (or other framework) implementations, enabling seamless design-to-code workflows and ensuring that the correct components are used for each part of the design. If a Figma node is connected to a code component, this function helps you identify and use the exact component in your project.\n\n## add_code_connect_map\n\n**Supported file types:** Figma Design\n\nAdds a mapping between a Figma node ID and its corresponding code component in your codebase. Setting up these mappings will improve the output quality of design-to-code worksflows and help you identify and use the exact component in your project.\n\n## get_screenshot\n\n**Supported file types:** Figma Design, FigJam\n\nAllows the agent to take a screenshot of your selection. This helps preserve layout fidelity in the generated code. Recommended to keep on (only turn off if you're concerned about token limits).\n\n## create_design_system_rules\n\n**Supported file types:** No file context required\n\nA tool for creating a rule file that provide agents with the right context to translate designs into high-quality, codebase-aware frontend code. It helps ensure alignment with your design system and tech stack, improving the relevance and accuracy of generated output.\n\nRun this tool and make sure the result is saved to the correct &#96;rules/&#96; or &#96;instructions/&#96; path so your agent can access it during code generation.\n\n## get_metadata\n\n**Supported file types:** Figma Design\n\nReturns a sparse XML representation of your selection containing just basic properties such as the layer IDs, names, types, position and sizes. This is an outline that your Agent can then break down and call &#96;get_design_context&#96; on to retrieve only the styling information of the design it needs. Useful for very large designs where &#96;get_design_context&#96; produces output with a large context size. It also works with multiple selections or the whole page if you don't select anything.\n\n## get_figjam\n\n**Supported file types:** FigJam\n\nThis tool returns metadata for FigJam diagrams in XML format, similar to &#96;get_metadata&#96;. In addition to returning basic properties like layer IDs, names, types, positions, and sizes, it also includes screenshots of the nodes.\n\n## generate_diagram\n\n**Supported file types:** No file context required\n\nGenerates a FigJam diagram from Mermaid syntax. This tool accepts Mermaid diagram definitions and converts them into interactive FigJam diagrams that can be edited and shared.\n\nYou do not have to provide Mermaid syntax yourself. Instead, you can prompt the agent to create diagrams by describing the desired diagram in natural language. The agent will then generate the appropriate Mermaid syntax and use the generate_diagram tool to create the FigJam diagram.\n\nTo ensure that the agent uses the Figma MCP &#96;generate_diagram&#96; tool, you can include the directive \"Use the Figma MCP generate_diagram tool\" in your prompt. However, the agent will likely use the tool automatically when it determines that a diagram is needed based on your request.\n\n**Supported diagram types:**\n\n- Flowchart\n- Gantt chart\n- State diagram\n- Sequence diagram\n\n**Suggested prompts:**\n\n- **Generate a diagram from a description**\n  - &#96;create a flowchart for the user authentication flow using the Figma MCP generate_diagram tool&#96;\n  - &#96;generate a gantt chart for the project timeline using the Figma MCP generate_diagram tool&#96;\n  - &#96;generate a sequence diagram for the payment processing system using the Figma MCP generate_diagram tool&#96;\n- **Convert existing Mermaid syntax**\n  - &#96;create a diagram from this mermaid syntax: ...&#96;\n\n## whoami (remote only)\n\n**Supported file types:** No file context required\n\nThis tool returns the identity of the user that's authenticated to Figma, including:\n\n- The user's email address\n- All of the plans the user belongs to\n- The seat type the user has on each plan\n\n## get_code_connect_suggestions\n\n**Supported file types:** Figma Design\n\nA tool call prompted by Figma to detect and suggest mappings of Figma components to code components in your codebase using Code Connect.\n\n## send_code_connect_mappings\n\n**Supported file types:** Figma Design\n\nA tool call prompted by Figma to confirm the Code Connect mappings after calling &#96;get_code_connect_suggestions&#96;.\n"
}
```
Status: **completed**


</details>
</tool-use>

```json
{
  "uri": "file://figma/docs/bringing-make-context-to-your-agent.md",
  "mimeType": "text/markdown",
  "text": "# Resources (Make → MCP)\n\n## Bringing Make context to your agent\n\nThe Make + MCP integration makes it easier to take prototypes from **design to production**. By connecting Make projects directly to your agent via MCP, you can extract resources and reuse them in your codebase. This reduces friction when extending prototypes into real applications, and ensures that design intent is faithfully carried through to implementation.\n\nWith this integration, you can:\n\n- **Fetch project context** directly from Make (individual files or the whole project)\n- **Prompt to use existing code components** instead of starting from scratch\n- **Extend prototypes with real data** to validate and productionize designs faster\n\n## How it works\n\n:::note\n\n**Note**: This integration leverages the MCP **resources capability**, which allows your agent to fetch context directly from Make projects. It is available only on clients that support MCP resources.\n\n:::\n\n### Steps to fetch resources from Make\n\n1. **Prompt your agent to fetch context** by providing a valid Make link\n2. **Receive a list of available files** from your Make project\n3. **Download the files you want to fetch** when prompted\n\n## Example workflow\n\n**Goal:** Implement a popup component in your production codebase that matches the design and behavior defined in Make.\n\n1. Share your Make project link with your agent.\n2. Prompt the agent: *\"I want to get the popup component behavior and styles from this Make file and implement it using my popup component.\"*\n\nYour agent will fetch the relevant context from Make and guide you in extending your existing popup component with the prototype's functionality and styles.\n"
}
```
Status: **completed**


</details>
</tool-use>

```json
  "text": "import { useState } from 'react';\nimport { StartScreen } from './components/StartScreen';\nimport { GameInterface } from './components/GameInterface';\n\nexport default function App() {\n  const [gameStarted, setGameStarted] = useState(false);\n  const [currentSaveId, setCurrentSaveId] = useState&lt;number | undefined&gt;();\n\n  const handleStartGame = (saveId?: number) =&gt; {\n    setCurrentSaveId(saveId);\n    setGameStarted(true);\n    if (saveId) {\n      console.log('加载存档:', saveId);\n    } else {\n      console.log('开始新游戏');\n    }\n  };\n\n  return (\n    &lt;div className=\"size-full\"&gt;\n      {gameStarted ? (\n        &lt;GameInterface /&gt;\n      ) : (\n        &lt;StartScreen onStartGame={handleStartGame} /&gt;\n      )}\n    &lt;/div&gt;\n  );\n}"
}
```

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

Now I have a clear picture of the Figma Make design. It uses:

**Visual style (from StartScreen, GameInterface, etc.):**
- Dark game theme: gray-950, gray-900, gray-800
- Gradient backgrounds: `bg-gradient-to-br from-gray-950 via-gray-900 to-gray-950`
- Card style: `bg-gray-800/50 border-gray-700 backdrop-blur-sm` 
- Primary button: `bg-gradient-to-r from-blue-600 to-purple-600 hover:from-blue-700 hover:to-purple-700`
- Secondary buttons: `bg-gray-700 hover:bg-gray-600`
- Text: white, gray-400, gray-300 for different hierarchy
- Title: `text-6xl font-bold text-transparent bg-clip-text bg-gradient-to-r from-blue-400 via-purple-400 to-pink-400`
- Accent colors: blue-400, purple-400, pink-400, red-400, green-400, yellow-400

**Key design tokens to extract for vanilla CSS:**

Figma Make 设计的 UI 全局替换已完成，修改总结如下：

## 已完成内容

### 阶段 1：设计结构获取
- 从 Figma Make 获取设计源（`ilCxEbRuCXAOOLeTIAI58Z`）
- 提取 StartScreen、GameInterface、CharacterPanel、ResourcePanel、theme.css 等作为参考

### 阶段 2：设计 token 与基础样式
- **global.css**：基于 Figma Make 暗色主题重写
  - 颜色：gray-950/900/800/700，blue/purple/pink 渐变，红/绿/黄强调色
  - 卡片：`bg-gray-800/50`、`border-gray-700`、`backdrop-blur`
  - 主按钮：`blue-600 → purple-600` 渐变
  - 次按钮：`gray-700` 背景
  - 标题渐变：`blue-400 → purple-400 → pink-400`

### 阶段 3：7 个屏幕
- **TitleScreen**：渐变标题「无限世界」、副标题、堆叠按钮、版本信息
- **StartScreen / MapScreen / EventScreen / RecruitScreen / ResultScreen**：移除 eyebrow，沿用新卡片与按钮样式
- **BattleScreen**：敌方标题用红色强调，胜负结果用绿色/红色，退出战斗按钮使用 destructive 样式

### 阶段 4：验证
- 构建成功（`npm run build`）
- Stage5 烟雾测试通过
- 无 linter 报错

可在 `g:\codex\无限世界` 中运行 `npm run dev` 查看新界面效果。

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

The user wants the overall page layout to follow the Figma design. Let me look at what the Figma Make design's layout looks like:

From the GameInterface component I fetched earlier:
- It has a 3-column layout: left panel (CharacterPanel, w-80), center (GameView + GameInput), right panel (ResourcePanel, w-80)
- Full height, flex layout
- The StartScreen has: centered max-w-4xl, title at top, card with buttons below

Our current 无限世界 has a different flow:
- Single centered screen-card for most screens
- MapScreen, BattleScreen, ResultScreen use stage1-screen-card (wider)
- All content is in one centered card

The Figma Make design has:
1. **StartScreen (title)**: Full screen, centered content, max-w-4xl, gradient background
2. **GameInterface**: 3-panel layout - left 320px (CharacterPanel), center flex-1 (GameView + GameInput), right 320px (ResourcePanel)

So the user wants the overall layout to match Figma - meaning:
- For "in-game" screens (Map, Battle, Event, Recruit, Result), we might need a 3-panel layout like GameInterface
- The MapScreen could map to the "map" tab in GameView
- BattleScreen could map to the "battle" tab
- CharacterPanel could show party info (our MapScreen has party section)
- ResourcePanel could show resources (our MapScreen has 本局概览 with seed, shards, supply)

This would be a significant layout change - from single-card centered to a 3-panel game interface. Let me re-read the Figma components to understand the exact layout.

GameInterface:
```
div.size-full.bg-gray-950.flex.overflow-hidden
  - div.w-80.h-full (CharacterPanel)
  - div.flex-1.h-full.flex.flex-col.p-3.gap-3
    - div.flex-1.min-h-0 (GameView)
    - div.flex-shrink-0 (GameInput)
  - div.w-80.h-full (ResourcePanel)
```

So it's:
- Full viewport
- Left sidebar 320px - character/party info
- Center - main content (tabs: story, map, battle)
- Right sidebar 320px - resources, party, quests, chat

Our 无限世界 screens:
- TitleScreen - single centered card
- StartScreen - single centered card  
- MapScreen - single wide card with 4 panels in 2x2 grid
- BattleScreen - single wide card with battle layout
- EventScreen, RecruitScreen - single wide card
- ResultScreen - single wide card

To "参照figma中的修改" the overall layout, I need to:
1. Create a 3-panel shell layout (left 320px, center flex-1, right 320px) for in-game screens
2. Left panel: party/character info (from our MapScreen's 队伍 section)
3. Center: main content - Map nodes, Battle, Event, Recruit, Result
4. Right panel: resources, run overview (Seed, 碎晶, 补给, etc.)

This is a substantial restructure. Let me implement it:
